assertion.statement: The authorized source record concerns a Commonwealth (AU-CTH) FOI matter.
assertion.evidence_window: captured request-page text only; source URL, archive timestamp, and provenance metadata establish identity but cannot alone support a label

labels.positive_rule: Use only when the authorized text explicitly identifies a Commonwealth FOI request or a Commonwealth agency FOI matter.
labels.negative_rule: Do not use for an inference from URL, agency name alone, page layout, or general Australian context.

labels.positive_rule: Use only when the text supplies explicit facts from which the assertion follows mechanically and the derivation is recorded.
labels.negative_rule: Do not use for legal interpretation, ambiguous jurisdiction, or a missing source statement.

labels.positive_rule: Use when the text supports a possible mapping but jurisdictional or temporal evidence is incomplete.
labels.negative_rule: Do not use when the mapping is contradicted or when the record is unreadable.

labels.positive_rule: Use when the supplied evidence cannot decide the assertion.
labels.negative_rule: Do not use as a substitute for a missing required span or as an implicit negative.

span_policy.boundary_rule: cite the shortest contiguous span that is sufficient for the label, preserving whole Unicode code points
span_policy.multiple_span_rule: at most three non-overlapping spans, sorted by start, only when one span cannot contain the explicit facts
span_policy.whole_document_span: invalid unless the entire document is 1000 characters or fewer and every character is necessary evidence

abstention.rule: abstention=true requires a reason and label=unknown; abstention=false requires a non-empty label and valid span unless label=unknown

serialization.null_label_means: unknown only when abstention=true and abstention_reason is present
